skill.name: video-downloader
skill.description: Downloads videos from YouTube and other platforms for offline viewing, editing, or archival. Handles various formats and quality options.
skill.summary: Guides video and audio downloads from supported platforms for permitted offline use.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, network, and weak cryptography patterns, but the cited content is Markdown examples and descriptive text rather than executable code. No prompt injection or malicious exfiltration behavior was found. The skill still carries medium operational risk because its intended behavior requires network downloads and local file output.

medium_findings.title: Network Download and Local File Output Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to download videos and audio from web platforms and save media files locally. This is expected behavior for the skill, but it can consume bandwidth and storage, and users must confirm permission, copyright status, and platform terms before downloading.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The stated purpose is downloading web videos to the computer, and the notes acknowledge copyright and permission constraints. This confirms legitimate but higher-risk network and filesystem behavior.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Video URLs in Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to example YouTube links in Markdown documentation. They are not hidden endpoints or evidence of data exfiltration, but they confirm that the skill is designed around user-provided network URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible examples in the How to Use and Example sections. There is no executable request code or suspicious destination.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: External Command Syntax in Markdown Blocks
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged multiple fenced Markdown examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The file contains no script implementation, and the fenced blocks contain natural language prompts and sample output rather than executable shell commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows the flagged content is documentation inside fenced code blocks. No executable Ruby, shell, or command runner is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The weak cryptography alerts cite the frontmatter description and metadata preservation text. These lines do not reference cryptographic algorithms or hashing APIs, so the static finding is dismissed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found ordinary descriptive text only. There is no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic usage.

content.value_statement: Saving permitted videos for offline work can be slow and inconsistent across platforms. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code plan video, audio, quality, and metadata download requests.
actual_capabilities: Helps request downloads for single videos from YouTube and similar platforms.
Supports prompts for specific quality targets such as 480p, 720p, 1080p, or 4K.
Supports audio-only requests for podcasts, talks, or music when permitted.
Describes batch and playlist download workflows for multiple media items.
Encourages saving metadata such as title, description, and thumbnail.
limitations: Does not include executable downloader code in the skill file.
Requires external tools or assistant environment support to perform real downloads.
Cannot bypass platform restrictions, digital rights controls, or account permissions.
Users remain responsible for copyright, fair use, and platform terms.

use_cases.title: Save Training Videos
use_cases.description: Download permitted tutorials before travel or low-connectivity work sessions.

use_cases.title: Archive Reference Media
use_cases.description: Preserve public webinars, talks, or owned recordings for research notes.

use_cases.title: Prepare Editing Assets
use_cases.description: Retrieve owned videos or authorized clips in a requested format for editing.

prompt_templates.title: Download One Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Download this permitted video for offline viewing: [video URL]. Save it to my Downloads folder.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Video Quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Specify file size and resolution needs
prompt_templates.prompt: Download this permitted video at 720p: [video URL]. Prefer MP4 if available and keep the file size reasonable.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Download the audio from this permitted video: [video URL]. Save it as an MP3 with the title in the filename.

prompt_templates.title: Archive a Playlist
prompt_templates.prompt: Download this permitted playlist: [playlist URL]. Use 1080p when available, save thumbnails, and organize files by playlist order.

output_examples.output: The assistant reports the video title, selected quality, download progress, final filename, thumbnail status, and saved location.

output_examples.input: Save only the audio from this lecture.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates an audio file, names it from the media title, and confirms the output folder.

output_examples.input: Archive these five approved training links.
output_examples.output: The assistant processes each link, reports successes and failures, and lists the saved files.

best_practices: Confirm that you own the media or have permission before downloading.
Specify resolution, format, and destination folder before starting large downloads.
Use audio-only mode when video is not needed to reduce storage use.
anti_patterns: Do not download copyrighted content without permission or a valid legal basis.
Do not use the skill to bypass access controls, paywalls, or platform restrictions.
Do not start large playlist downloads without checking available disk space.

faq.question: Does this skill include a downloader program?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides prompts and workflow guidance. Real downloads require compatible tools in the assistant environment.

faq.question: Can it download from YouTube?
faq.answer: It is written for YouTube and similar platforms, subject to permission, tool support, and platform terms.

faq.question: Can I choose the video quality?
faq.answer: Yes. The prompts support quality requests such as 480p, 720p, 1080p, and 4K when available.

faq.question: Can it save audio only?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes audio-only requests for permitted media, such as lectures or podcasts.

faq.question: Is this safe for copyrighted videos?
faq.answer: Only use it when you have permission, own the content, or have a valid legal basis.

faq.question: Will it preserve metadata?
faq.answer: The workflow can request titles, descriptions, and thumbnails when the download tool supports them.
